key_themes: Consistent EPS growth, earnings beats, and resilient fundamentals
Hurricane-related cost pressures weighing on near-term margins
Bullish analyst consensus with average price target ~$537–$543 (10–12% upside)
Strong operational recognitions (top hospitals, ethics awards) and capital returns (buybacks, dividend hike)
Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings (April 24) as key catalyst with solid expectations
summary: HCA Healthcare maintains positive financial sentiment driven by robust long-term growth (16% annual EPS growth, consistent beats, $76B+ revenue scale) and predominantly Buy analyst ratings with an average 12-month price target around $537–$543 from current levels near $488.[[1]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/HCA/forecast/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HCA/) Cost pressures from recent hurricanes have prompted some caution, profit warnings, and a pullback from 52-week highs (~$556), alongside a recent Baird PT cut to $442.[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HCA/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/HCA/) Social/investor discussion on X is relatively quiet and neutral, focused on the upcoming April 24 Q1 2026 earnings (expected EPS ~$7.19). Overall outlook remains constructive on operational scale, quality metrics, and capital deployment.[[3]](https://investor.hcahealthcare.com/news/default.aspx)
sources: 25+ news articles and analyst reports (Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, TipRanks, Seeking Alpha, company IR), 15+ recent X posts (primarily earnings calendar mentions, limited direct sentiment; one PT cut and one rating upgrade noted)
